briefdescription.para: Device hardware is powered, and the device is needed by the system.

detaileddescription.para: The device should be enabled in this state. Any device driver API may be called in this state.

briefdescription.para: Device hardware is powered, but the device is not needed by the system.

detaileddescription.para: The device should be put into its lowest internal power state, commonly named "disabled" or "stopped".

If a device has been specified as this device's power domain, and said device is no longer needed by the system, this device will be transitioned into the PM_DEVICE_STATE_OFF state, followed by the power domain device being transitioned to the PM_DEVICE_STATE_SUSPENDED state.

A device driver may be deinitialized in this state. Once the device driver has been deinitialized, we implicitly move to the PM_DEVICE_STATE_OFF state as the device hardware may lose power, with no device driver to respond to the corresponding PM_DEVICE_ACTION_TURN_OFF action.

simplesect.para: This state is NOT a "low-power"/"partially operable" state, those are configured using device driver specific APIs, and apply only while the device is in the PM_DEVICE_STATE_ACTIVE state.

briefdescription.para: Device hardware is powered, but the device has been scheduled to be suspended, as it is no longer needed by the system.

detaileddescription.para: This state is used when delegating suspension of a device to the PM subsystem, optionally with residency to avoid unnecessary suspend/resume cycles, resulting from a call to pm_device_runtime_put_async. The device will be unscheduled in case the device becomes needed by the system.
No device driver API calls must occur in this state.

simplesect.para: that this state is opaque to the device driver (no pm_device_action is called as this state is entered) and is used solely by PM_DEVICE_RUNTIME.

briefdescription.para: Device hardware is not powered.

detaileddescription.para: This is the initial state from which a device driver is initialized.

When a device driver is initialized, we do not know the state of the device. As a result, the PM_DEVICE_ACTION_TURN_ON action should be able to transition the device from any internal state into PM_DEVICE_STATE_SUSPENDED, since no guarantees can be made across resets. This is typically achieved through toggling a reset pin or triggering a software reset through a register write before performing any additional configuration needed to meet the requirements of PM_DEVICE_STATE_SUSPENDED. For devices where this is not possible, the device driver must presume the device is in either the PM_DEVICE_STATE_OFF or PM_DEVICE_STATE_SUSPENDED state at time of initialization, as these are the states within which device drivers may be deinitialized.

If a device has been specified as this device's power domain, and said device becomes needed by the system, the power domain device will be transitioned into the PM_DEVICE_STATE_ACTIVE state, followed by this device being transitioned to the PM_DEVICE_STATE_SUSPENDED state.

briefdescription.para: Device power states.
Suspend.
Resume.
Turn off.

detaileddescription.para: Action triggered only by a power domain.

This action serves as a notification to the device that its power domain is about to be powered off. After this action completes, the device must have disabled IRQs, released resources, and be fully prepared for power loss.

The actual power domain power-off timing is platform-specific and may occur immediately, be deferred, or be handled as part of a system-level power transition. This action only indicates that the device should prepare for potential power loss.

briefdescription.para: Turn on.

detaileddescription.para: Action triggered only by a power domain.
This action serves as a notification to the device that its power domain has been powered on.

briefdescription.para: Device PM actions.

memberdef.definition: typedef int(* pm_device_action_cb_t) (const struct device *dev, enum pm_device_action action)
memberdef.name: pm_device_action_cb_t

briefdescription.para: Device PM action callback.

parameterdescription.para: If the requested action is not supported.

memberdef.definition: typedef bool(* pm_device_action_failed_cb_t) (const struct device *dev, int err)
memberdef.name: pm_device_action_failed_cb_t

briefdescription.para: Device PM action failed callback.

simplesect.para: True to continue iteration, false to halt iteration.

briefdescription.para: Get name of device PM state.

parameterdescription.para: State id which name should be returned

briefdescription.para: Run a pm action on a device.

detaileddescription.para: This function calls the device PM control callback so that the device does the necessary operations to execute the given action.

parameterdescription.para: If requested state is not supported.
If device is already at the requested state.
If device is changing its state.
If device does not support PM.
If device has power state locked.

memberdef.argsstring: (const struct device *dev, enum pm_device_action action, pm_device_action_failed_cb_t failure_cb)
memberdef.name: pm_device_children_action_run

briefdescription.para: Run a pm action on all children of a device.

detaileddescription.para: This function calls all child devices PM control callback so that the device does the necessary operations to execute the given action.

parameterdescription.para: Function to call if a child fails the action, can be NULL.

briefdescription.para: Obtain the power state of a device.

parameterdescription.para: Pointer where device power state will be stored.
If device does not implement power management.

briefdescription.para: Initialize a device state to PM_DEVICE_STATE_SUSPENDED.

detaileddescription.para: By default device state is initialized to PM_DEVICE_STATE_ACTIVE. However in order to save power some drivers may choose to only initialize the device to the suspended state, or actively put the device into the suspended state. This function can therefore be used to notify the PM subsystem that the device is in PM_DEVICE_STATE_SUSPENDED instead of the default.

briefdescription.para: Initialize a device state to PM_DEVICE_STATE_OFF.

detaileddescription.para: By default device state is initialized to PM_DEVICE_STATE_ACTIVE. In general, this makes sense because the device initialization function will resume and configure a device, leaving it operational. However, when power domains are enabled, the device may be connected to a switchable power source, in which case it won't be powered at boot. This function can therefore be used to notify the PM subsystem that the device is in PM_DEVICE_STATE_OFF instead of the default.

briefdescription.para: Mark a device as busy.

detaileddescription.para: Devices marked as busy will not be suspended when the system goes into low-power states. This can be useful if, for example, the device is in the middle of a transaction.

briefdescription.para: Clear a device busy status.
Check if any device is busy.

parameterdescription.para: If one or more devices are busy

briefdescription.para: Check if a device is busy.

parameterdescription.para: If the device is not busy

briefdescription.para: Enable or disable a device as a wake up source.

detaileddescription.para: A device marked as a wake up source will not be suspended when the system goes into low-power modes, thus allowing to use it as a wake up source for the system.

parameterdescription.para: true to enable or false to disable
If the wakeup source was successfully enabled.
If the wakeup source was not successfully enabled.

briefdescription.para: Check if a device is enabled as a wake up source.

parameterdescription.para: if the wakeup source is enabled.
if the wakeup source is not enabled.

briefdescription.para: Check if a device is wake up capable.

parameterdescription.para: If the device is wake up capable.
If the device is not wake up capable.

briefdescription.para: Check if the device is on a switchable power domain.

parameterdescription.para: If device is on a switchable power domain.
If device is not on a switchable power domain.

briefdescription.para: Add a device to a power domain.

detaileddescription.para: This function adds a device to a given power domain.

parameterdescription.para: Device to be added to the power domain.
If device is already part of the power domain.
If the application was built without power domain support.
If there is no space available in the power domain to add the device.

briefdescription.para: Remove a device from a power domain.

detaileddescription.para: This function removes a device from a given power domain.

parameterdescription.para: Device to be removed from the power domain.
If the application was built without power domain support.
If device is not in the given domain.

briefdescription.para: Check if the device is currently powered.

parameterdescription.para: If device is currently powered, or is assumed to be powered (i.e. it does not support PM or is not under a PM domain)
If device is not currently powered

briefdescription.para: Move a device driver into its initial device power state.

detaileddescription.para: This function uses the device driver's internal PM hook to move the device from the OFF state to the initial power state expected by the system.
The initial power state expected by the system is:

listitem.para: ACTIVE if CONFIG_PM_DEVICE=n or (CONFIG_PM_DEVICE=y and CONFIG_PM_DEVICE_RUNTIME=n) or (CONFIG_PM_DEVICE_RUNTIME=y and !pm_device_runtime_is_enabled(dev)).
SUSPENDED if CONFIG_PM_DEVICE_RUNTIME=y and device's parent power domain is ACTIVE.
OFF if CONFIG_PM_DEVICE_RUNTIME=y and device's parent power domain is SUSPENDED.

simplesect.para: This function must be called at the end of a driver's init function.

parameterdescription.para: Error code from action_cb on failure.

briefdescription.para: Prepare PM device for device driver deinit.

detaileddescription.para: Ensures device is either SUSPENDED or OFF. If CONFIG_PM_DEVICE=y, the function checks whether power management has moved the device to either the SUSPENDED or OFF states. If CONFIG_PM_DEVICE=n, the function uses the device driver's internal PM hook to move the device to the SUSPENDED state.

simplesect.para: This function must be called at the beginning of a driver's deinit function.

parameterdescription.para: Device is not SUSPENDED nor OFF

briefdescription.para: Flag indicating that runtime PM API for the device can be called from any context.

detaileddescription.para: If PM_DEVICE_ISR_SAFE flag is used for device definition, it indicates that PM actions are synchronous and can be executed from any context. This approach can be used for cases where suspending and resuming is short as it is executed in the critical section. This mode requires less resources (~80 byte less RAM) and allows to use device runtime PM from any context (including interrupts).

briefdescription.para: Define device PM resources for the given device name.

simplesect.para: This macro is a no-op if CONFIG_PM_DEVICE is not enabled.

parameterdescription.para: Optional flag to indicate that ISR safe. Use PM_DEVICE_ISR_SAFE or 0.

briefdescription.para: Define device PM resources for the given node identifier.

simplesect.para: This macro is a no-op if CONFIG_PM_DEVICE is not enabled.

parameterdescription.para: Optional flag to indicate that device is isr_ok. Use PM_DEVICE_ISR_SAFE or 0.

briefdescription.para: Define device PM resources for the given instance.

simplesect.para: This macro is a no-op if CONFIG_PM_DEVICE is not enabled.

parameterdescription.para: Optional flag to indicate that device is isr_ok. Use PM_DEVICE_ISR_SAFE or 0.

briefdescription.para: Obtain a reference to the device PM resources for the given device.

simplesect.para: Reference to the device PM resources (NULL if device CONFIG_PM_DEVICE is disabled).

briefdescription.para: Obtain a reference to the device PM resources for the given node.

simplesect.para: Reference to the device PM resources (NULL if device CONFIG_PM_DEVICE is disabled).

briefdescription.para: Obtain a reference to the device PM resources for the given instance.

simplesect.para: Reference to the device PM resources (NULL if device CONFIG_PM_DEVICE is disabled).

briefdescription.para: Device Power Management API.
